text:
Perfect! Now you have a custom git command. Here's how to use it:

```bash
git quickpush "your commit message"
```

This command will:
1. Add all changes (`git add .`)
2. Commit with your message
3. Push to remote if it exists
4. Show you success/error messages

**Note**: The first time you push to a new repository, git will ask for your credentials. After that, git will cache them securely so you won't need to enter them again.

If you need to set up a remote repository later, use:
```bash
git remote add origin <your-repository-url>
```

Your custom `git quickpush` command is ready to use!
